creator: Alexander Terp
description:
CGo (via tree-sitter) is a recurring source of distribution pain: no 'go install', cross-compilation needs C cross-compilers, release pipeline is the only practical source of interpreters (relevant to RED-D's compile feature).

Idea: eliminate CGo by replacing the tree-sitter C runtime with a Go-native one. Scope is smaller than it sounds:
- Port only the *runtime* (GLR stack, error recovery, incremental reparse; libtree-sitter ~15-20K LOC of C). The generator and grammar.js workflow stay as-is; tree-sitter-rad keeps serving editors unchanged.
- parser.c (217K lines) is almost entirely generated tables - mechanically transpilable to Go data.
- scanner.c is 866 hand-written lines we own - port by hand.
- Frozen-by-choice is fine: we don't need upstream feature velocity, just ABI pinning.

Verification: C implementation remains a perfect differential oracle forever. Keep both runtimes behind a build flag; require byte-identical CSTs across the full snapshot corpus + fuzzed inputs + fuzzed edit sequences (error recovery and incremental reparse are where snapshots alone are thinnest). Strong fit for AI-agent-driven porting given the tight verification loop.

Cheaper alternative to evaluate first: compile grammar+runtime to WASM, run via wazero (pure-Go). ~Week-long spike vs months-long port; ~2-3x parse slowdown (likely irrelevant at script sizes, measure for radls). Cuts against own-your-deps ethos but de-risks and could serve as interim step.

Next step when picked up: wazero spike + parse benchmarks on real rad scripts, then write a RED ('eliminate CGo') with port-vs-wasm-vs-status-quo as alternatives.

Not a prerequisite for RED-D (appending payloads to CGo binaries works fine) - it softens RED-D's compile costs but sequences independently.
title: Pure-Go tree-sitter runtime (eliminate CGo)
